summary: Machine policy for Microsoft feedback prompts. Microsoft documents the same device policy path, value name, default, and numeric meanings that the app writes.

setting.name: Microsoft Feedback Notifications
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can occasionally show prompts asking for feedback. This policy decides whether those feedback questions appear on the device.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable feedback prompts to reduce interruptions, prevent Microsoft feedback requests on managed devices, or keep the UI quieter.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces prompts and distractions, but it also removes a built-in way for Windows to request feedback from the user.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications. FeedbackNotifications.admx maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the feedback-notification policy.

allowed_values.label: Feedback notifications allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Windows feedback prompts may be shown.

allowed_values.label: Feedback notifications disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows feedback prompts are blocked by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the policy baseline and not a claim about every regional or edition-specific feedback experience.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices where occasional Microsoft feedback requests are acceptable
avoid_for: Managed systems that should stay quiet
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal policy behavior for feedback prompts.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to suppress Microsoft feedback requests.
tradeoffs: Windows can continue showing feedback questions.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet managed
avoid_for: Users who want Windows to prompt for feedback

states.rationale: Block Microsoft feedback prompts by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows feedback requests suppressed on the device.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want an easy built-in path for Windows feedback prompts to appear.
tradeoffs: Users are less likely to see a built-in invitation to send Microsoft feedback.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that prevents Windows devices from showing Microsoft feedback questions.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FeedbackNotifications.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the DataCollection policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications policy
evidence.summary: The privacy mirror lists DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications under the DataCollection policy family with the exact PolicyName and ValueName. The policy name indicates Microsoft Feedback Notifications are suppressed; the mirrored metadata keeps the registry surface tied to the value name used by the app.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reversible probe - DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications 0/1 toggle
evidence.summary: The guest probe wrote DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications=0 and DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications=1, queried the live registry after each write, opened the feedback settings surface in both states, and restored the original state.

decision.why: Microsoft documents DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications directly in the Experience Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications with 0 as the default not-disabled state and 1 as disabled. The app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection; Registry Value Name DoNotShowFeedbackNotifications; 0 (Default) Feedback notifications aren't disabled ... 1 Feedback notifications are disabled.
validation_proof.notes: The Experience Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes and defines the 0/1 semantics. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-donotshowfeedbacknotifications-admx. Win25H2Clean reversible probe at H:\Temp\vm-tooling-staging\feedback_notifications_probe.txt confirmed writes for 0 and 1, live registry queries after each write, feedback settings surface opens in both states, and restoration to the original state.
